input.command:
for file in ./src/index.rs ./src/parser/c_lang.rs ./src/parser/cpp.rs ./src/parser/csharp.rs ./src/parser/go.rs ./src/parser/java.rs ./src/parser/javascript.rs ./src/parser/python.rs ./src/parser/ruby.rs ./src/parser/rust_lang.rs ./src/parser/treesitter.rs ./src/parser/typescript.rs; do
  # Check if the file has parse_file calls outside of test blocks
  # Look for parse_file calls that are not in #[test] sections
  if grep -q "parse_file\s*(" "$file"; then
    # Count lines before #[cfg(test)] that have parse_file
    total_lines=$(wc -l < "$file")
    test_line=$(grep -n "#\[cfg(test)\]" "$file" | tail -1 | cut -d: -f1)
    
    if [ -z "$test_line" ]; then
      test_line=$((total_lines + 1))
    fi
    
    # Check if there are parse_file calls before the test section
    if sed -n "1,$((test_line-1))p" "$file" | grep -q "parse_file\s*("; then
      echo "$file"
    fi
  fi
done

input.command:
# Get the list of files with parse_file calls
# Exclude test cases by checking if the call is before #[cfg(test)]

declare -a files_with_calls

# Check index.rs
if grep -q "crate::parser::treesitter::parse_file(" ./src/index.rs; then
  files_with_calls+=("./src/index.rs")
fi

# Check treesitter.rs for parse_file calls (not inside tests)
if grep "parse_file\s*(" ./src/parser/treesitter.rs | grep -v "fn parse_file" | head -1 | grep -q "match parse_file"; then
  files_with_calls+=("./src/parser/treesitter.rs")
fi

# Check if any other files have calls (not just tests)
# For parser files like c_lang, cpp, etc., most calls are in tests
# Let's verify by checking if parse_file appears before the first #[cfg(test)]

for parser_file in ./src/parser/{c_lang,cpp,csharp,go,java,javascript,python,ruby,rust_lang,typescript}.rs; do
  if [ -f "$parser_file" ]; then
    # Get the line number of the first test section
    test_line=$(grep -n "#\[cfg(test)\]" "$parser_file" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1)
    
    if [ -z "$test_line" ]; then
      # No test section found, so all parse_file calls are non-test code
      if grep -q "parse_file\s*(" "$parser_file"; then
        files_with_calls+=("$parser_file")
      fi
    else
      # Check if there are parse_file calls before test section
      if sed -n "1,$((test_line-1))p" "$parser_file" | grep -q "parse_file\s*("; then
        files_with_calls+=("$parser_file")
      fi
    fi
  fi
done

printf '%s\n' "${files_with_calls[@]}" | sort
